title: AI Procurement Decision Card
description: A buyer-side artifact recording the outcome of a procurement review of one or more AI vendor declaration documents. Served at /.well-known/decisions/{decision_id}.json (per-decision) or referenced in /.well-known/decisions/index.json. v0.3 draft (v0.1 and v0.2 documents remain valid). v0.2 added data_vault_targets; v0.3 adds retention_envelope for per-field TTL + signed deletion proofs.

decision_card_version.description: Spec version. '0.1', '0.2', or '0.3'. v0.2 adds the optional 'data_vault_targets' field for declaring vendor-neutral PII tokenization vault targets the decision authorizes at runtime. v0.3 adds the optional 'retention_envelope' field for declaring per-field TTLs and signed deletion-proof endpoints. Earlier versions remain valid against this schema.

decision_id.description: Stable, buyer-issued identifier. Conventionally DEC-<year>-<seq> but any string unique to the buyer is valid.

issued_at.description: RFC 3339 timestamp the decision was published.

conditions.description: Conditions attached to an approval, or remediation requirements for a rejection. Required when decision.status is 'approved-with-conditions' or 'rejected-with-remediation'.

rationale.description: Narrative explanation of the decision in human-readable prose.

history.description: Audit trail of state transitions during the review.

data_vault_targets.description: v0.2. Optional. Vendor-neutral declaration of PII tokenization vault targets this decision authorizes at runtime — used by the policy-as-code engine and downstream enforcement points (MCP permission broker, governance bridges, RAG indexers) to route specific PII fields through a vault before persistence and to permit detokenization only for declared reveal_roles. Vendor-neutral by design: the 'vendor' enum names known providers but the field is shape-only; pricing, contracts, and provider selection live outside this spec.

retention_envelope.description: v0.3. Optional. Per-field retention rules the buyer attaches to the decision: how long each PII field may be stored, what redaction action fires at expiry, where the signed deletion receipt is posted, and which roles may hold a field past its TTL (legal-hold exemptions). Designed as the natural pairing with data_vault_targets — vault targets say WHO can read, retention envelope says HOW LONG the underlying data lives and HOW deletion is proven. Vendor-neutral and additive: enforcement implementations (data-contract-registry, audit-stream consumers, vault tools) decide how to act on the rules; this spec only declares the contract.

jurisdiction.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code, optionally with a region (e.g. 'US-CA', 'GB', 'EU').

id.description: Stable canonical identifier for the buyer (typically a URI).

role.description: Title or role of the individual or committee that made the decision.

authority.description: The formal authority under which the decision was made — e.g. 'Board Resolution 2026-04', 'CMO delegation'.

effective_from.description: Date from which the decision takes effect (ISO 8601 date or date-time).

effective_until.description: Date the decision expires. Decisions with this field set transition to status 'expired' automatically after this date.

scope.description: Free-form description of where/how the approval applies — e.g. 'K-12 classroom use during regular school hours', 'cardiology imaging only'.

vendor_id.description: Stable canonical identifier for the vendor (typically their AI Entity Card URL).

documents_reviewed.description: Vendor declaration documents (AEO entity card, Tool Disclosure, Clinical AI Card, etc.) considered during the review.

fetched_at.description: RFC 3339 timestamp the document was retrieved for this review.

content_hash.description: Hash of the canonical-form document content. Format: <alg>:<hex>. SHA-256 is recommended.

version.description: Vendor-published version string of the reviewed document, if available.

policy_uris.description: URIs of buyer-side policy documents the review was conducted against (e.g. the buyer's own AUP, RFP requirements, regulatory checklist).

enforcement.description: How compliance with the condition is enforced.

violation_response.description: What happens if the condition is violated.

verification_uri.description: URL where compliance evidence can be reviewed.

visibility_notes.description: Free-form notes on the publication policy — e.g. 'redacted version published; full version available on request'.

key_uri.description: URL where the public key for verification can be retrieved (for cryptographic signatures).

signature_value.description: The signature value itself, base64-encoded (for cryptographic signatures).

replaces.description: If this withdrawn decision is being replaced by a new one, the new decision_id.

DataVaultTarget.description: v0.2. One PII tokenization vault target this decision authorizes. The decision_card_version='0.2' document declares ONLY the contract (vendor, vault id/uri, fields, reveal_roles). The actual vault, its credentials, its key material, and its commercial relationship sit outside this spec — buyer and vendor implementations choose those at deployment time.

vendor.description: Vault provider. Vendor-neutral enum; 'custom' covers self-hosted vaults and 'other' covers providers not yet listed. Adding a name here is descriptive only; the spec does not endorse any provider.

vault_id.description: Provider-issued vault identifier. Format is vendor-specific.

vault_url.description: Public reference URL for the vault — e.g. tenant URL, provider console URL, or runbook URL where reviewers can audit the vault configuration.

fields_authorized.description: Names of the PII fields this decision authorizes for tokenization through this vault. Simple field names ('email', 'phone', 'ssn') or JSONPath-style selectors ('$.contact.email'). The buyer is responsible for keeping this list aligned with the vendor's declared collected_pii fields.

reveal_roles.description: Roles that may detokenize (reveal) the vaulted PII when this decision is in force. Role names are buyer-defined — e.g. 'fraud-investigator', 'support-l2', 'auditor'. Empty array means no role may reveal; the field stays tokenized end-to-end.

reveal_audit_uri.description: URL where reveal events should be appended for tamper-evident audit (e.g. an evidence-bundle endpoint or an audit-stream service).

expires_at.description: Optional override. If absent, the vault authorization expires with the decision (decision.effective_until).

notes.description: Free-form notes — e.g. residency requirements, key-rotation expectations, contractual carve-outs.

RetentionRule.description: v0.3. One retention rule attached to a field (or a JSONPath-style selector). Buyer-declared: this decision authorizes the named field for the specified TTL, then the named redaction action fires, and a signed receipt is posted to deletion_proof_uri so an auditor can verify deletion happened.

field.description: Field name or JSONPath-style selector this rule applies to. Same conventions as DataVaultTarget.fields_authorized — simple names ('email') or selectors ('$.contact.email'). Use '*' to apply the rule to every PII field declared in the matching data_vault_targets entry.

ttl.description: Time-to-live. ISO 8601 duration ('P30D', 'P7Y', 'P12M') or an absolute RFC 3339 timestamp at which retention expires. Durations are computed from the value's first persistence, not from the decision's issued_at.

redact_on_expiry.description: Action that fires when the TTL elapses. 'purge' = remove the value entirely. 'tokenize' = replace with a vault token but keep the row (requires a matching data_vault_targets entry). 'hash' = replace with a one-way SHA-256 hash so cohort analysis is possible without identifiers. 'replace-with-null' = zero the field but keep the schema slot.

deletion_proof_uri.description: URL where the signed deletion receipt is posted after redact_on_expiry fires. Receipts follow the audit-stream event shape (canonical-JSON SHA-256, ed25519 signed) and can be appended to an audit-stream-py instance for tamper-evident long-term storage.

deletion_signer_key_uri.description: Public-key URL for verifying deletion receipts. The same key may be used across many retention rules. Convention: a JWK Set served at /.well-known/keys/retention-signer.json.

exemptions.description: Roles or operational triggers that may hold the field past its TTL — typically legal-hold, active-litigation, or regulator-request. Each exemption pauses the TTL clock; the exemption itself is auditable via the audit-stream.

notes.description: Free-form notes — e.g. 'extended to P12M while NCES study CDC-2026-117 is open', 'aligned with district board policy 5125'.

RetentionExemption.description: v0.3. One exemption that pauses TTL accrual for a retention rule. Declarative only — actual enforcement (matching incoming role claims, recording the hold start, releasing on hold lift) lives in the consumer.

trigger.description: Why the field is held. 'active-legal-hold' covers active litigation or e-discovery. 'regulator-request' covers a supervisory authority's preservation order. 'active-investigation' covers internal investigations. 'consent-renewal-pending' covers a renewal still in flight. 'other' is a catch-all — buyer notes the specific basis in notes.

role.description: Buyer-declared role authorized to invoke this exemption (e.g. 'legal-hold-officer', 'compliance-officer'). Matches the same role vocabulary as DataVaultTarget.reveal_roles. Empty = anyone with appropriate authority on the underlying system.

max_extension.description: Optional cap on how long the exemption may pause TTL accrual. ISO 8601 duration ('P30D') or absolute RFC 3339 timestamp. When set and the cap elapses, the rule's redact_on_expiry fires regardless of the hold.

audit_uri.description: URL where invocation/release events for this exemption should be posted (typically the same audit-stream as deletion_proof_uri).

notes.description: Free-form notes — e.g. case number, regulator reference, or specific carve-out language.
